headline: Letter: Snoopy, the financial crisis and a shooting in Sarajevo
summary: From Duncan Stephenson, Leeds, West Yorkshire, UK
body_text:
It seems to be symptomatic of our times that the front page “basement” story in last week’s FT Weekend edition should reveal the shock news that Snoopy and Charlie Brown were heading for Japan (Report, FT Weekend, December 20), whereas confirmation of Arctic winter sea ice reached its lowest maximum extent on record in 2025 appeared at the foot of the back page, in the “NikkeiAsia — the voice of the Asian century” slot.

Or perhaps it is an example of what the Prudential Regulation Authority, in the wake of the 2008 financial crisis, referred to as “disaster myopia”?

Anyway, it reminded me of the Al Stewart song “Manuscript” (on the album Zero She Flies) in which the Tsar in his great Winter Palace calls for the foreign news and is told: “An archduke was shot down in Bosnia, but nothing much.”

Duncan StephensonLeeds, West Yorkshire, UK
